headline: Reliable Robotics Announces $160M in New Investment
summary: MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., April 21, 2026--Reliable Robotics, the leader in autonomous aircraft systems, today announced $160M in new funding to accelerate deployment and scale production of the Reliable Autonomy System (RAS) – the first Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) certifiable system enabling fully automated operation of an aircraft. The financing follows commitments for over 200 systems from commercial and military customers, and selection for the United States Department of Transportatio

The financing follows commitments for over 200 systems from commercial and military customers, and selection for the United States Department of Transportatio

headline: Fed Chair Hearing, Tesla Earnings: What to Watch This Week
summary: Earnings season kicks into high gear this week, with nearly 20% of the S&P 500 scheduled to report quarterly earnings. Big names reporting include Tesla, Boeing and Intel. Investors will also watch the scheduled testimony Tuesday of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair.

content.title: Fed Chair Hearing, Tesla Earnings: What to Watch This Week
content.summary: Earnings season kicks into high gear this week, with nearly 20% of the S&P 500 scheduled to report quarterly earnings. Big names reporting include Tesla, Boeing and Intel. Investors will also watch the scheduled testimony Tuesday of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair.

headline: Ethiopian Airlines Converts Options of Six Boeing 787 Dreamliner Airplanes into Firm Orders
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and Ethiopian Airlines announced today the purchase of six 787 Dreamliner jets as the airline fully exercises commitments from its 2023 landmark order. Ethiopian Airlines will use the 787-9 jets to expand its intercontinental network from Addis Ababa and increase cargo capacity as demand for long-haul travel continues to rise.

content.title: Ethiopian Airlines Converts Options of Six Boeing 787 Dreamliner Airplanes into Firm Orders
content.summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and Ethiopian Airlines announced today the purchase of six 787 Dreamliner jets as the airline fully exercises commitments from its 2023 landmark order. Ethiopian Airlines will use the 787-9 jets to expand its intercontinental network from Addis Ababa and increase cargo capacity as demand for long-haul travel continues to rise.

thumbnail.caption: Pictured from left to right at a signing ceremony at Boeing's office in Arlington, Va.: Anbessie Yitbarek, Vice President of Commercial Sales and Marketing for Africa, Boeing; Ato Mesfin Tasew, Group Chief Executive Officer of Ethiopian Airlines; Mr. Hiro Rodriguez, Executive Director of the Advocacy Center at the U.S. Department of Commerce; Ms. Sarah Whitten, Chief Commercial Officer and Head of Origination at the U.S. Export-Import Bank; Ms. Sarah Troutman, U.S. Deputy Assistant Secretary of State; H.E. Ahmed Shide, Minister of Finance of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia; H.E. Binalf Andualem, Ambassador of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia to the U.S.; Teklewold Atnafu, Advisor to the Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia; Mr. Temesgen Getaye, Treasurer of Ethiopian Airlines; Mr. Abraham Tesfaye, Director of Infrastructure Planning and Development, Ethiopian Airlines.

headline: Robot.com Appoints Gregory D. Smith to Board of Directors
summary: Robot.com, the autonomous robotics company, today announced the appointment of Gregory D. Smith to its board of directors as an observer. Smith currently serves as Chairman of American Airlines' board and is the co-founder of G2 Equity Partners.

headline: Boeing Earnings Are Coming. Can They Avoid the Aerospace ‘Bloodbath?’
summary: For the quarter, Wall Street projects a per share loss of 68 cents from sales of $21.3 billion, according to FactSet.  A year ago, Boeing reported a per share loss of 49 cents from sales of $19.5 billion.  Boeing delivered 143 commercial planes in the first three months of the year, up from 130 a year ago.

content.title: Boeing Earnings Are Coming. Can They Avoid the Aerospace ‘Bloodbath?’
content.summary: For the quarter, Wall Street projects a per share loss of 68 cents from sales of $21.3 billion, according to FactSet.  A year ago, Boeing reported a per share loss of 49 cents from sales of $19.5 billion.  Boeing delivered 143 commercial planes in the first three months of the year, up from 130 a year ago.

headline: Tesla, Boeing, IBM, Southwest earnings: What to Watch
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come tomorrow, Wednesday, April 22, including a torrent of earnings results from various companies that include GE Vernova (GEV), Boeing (BA), AT&T (T), Tesla (TSLA), International Business Machines (IBM), Texas Instruments (TXN), ServiceNow (NOW), and Southwest Airlines (LUV).
